raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.7,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.5",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Confirm editing scope: A) light copyedit only (grammar, style, wording) or B) substantive edit (reframing contributions, reorganizing flow, tightening logic, moderating claims)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Refining the high-level thesis and contribution framing can clarify what is being claimed and why it matters.",
    "R1.2": "Checking the argument from setup to results to conclusions helps ensure the reasoning is coherent and defensible.",
    "R1.3": "Verifying that claims match the reported metrics and observations avoids misalignment between evidence and assertions.",
    "R1.5": "Improving the abstract’s narrative flow (problem → method → results → implications) can increase clarity and impact.",
    "R1.6": "Sentence-level polishing will enhance clarity, concision, and academic tone.",
    "RX.1": "Edits should signal uncertainty and limits of generalization where appropriate.",
    "RX.2": "Wording must avoid overstating generality beyond the presented experiments.",
    "RX.3": "Work should remain within improving the provided draft without adding new external content or experiments.",
    "RX.4": "Careful phrasing reduces risk of misleading practitioners into overconfident deployment or conclusions.",
    "RX.5": "Changes should preserve traceability to original claims and make suggested edits clearly attributable."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Confirm editing scope: A) light copyedit only (grammar, style, wording) or B) substantive edit (reframing contributions, reorganizing flow, tightening logic, moderating claims)?

rationale.R1.1: Refining the high-level thesis and contribution framing can clarify what is being claimed and why it matters.
rationale.R1.2: Checking the argument from setup to results to conclusions helps ensure the reasoning is coherent and defensible.
rationale.R1.3: Verifying that claims match the reported metrics and observations avoids misalignment between evidence and assertions.
rationale.R1.5: Improving the abstract’s narrative flow (problem → method → results → implications) can increase clarity and impact.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence-level polishing will enhance clarity, concision, and academic tone.
rationale.RX.1: Edits should signal uncertainty and limits of generalization where appropriate.
rationale.RX.2: Wording must avoid overstating generality beyond the presented experiments.
rationale.RX.3: Work should remain within improving the provided draft without adding new external content or experiments.
rationale.RX.4: Careful phrasing reduces risk of misleading practitioners into overconfident deployment or conclusions.
rationale.RX.5: Changes should preserve traceability to original claims and make suggested edits clearly attributable.
